meaning: adj.鍚夋櫘璧涗汉[璇璢,鍚夋櫘璧涗汉涔嬮淇椾範鎯殑
examples: The Gypsies speak a language called Romany which has many common words with Indian languages.
Last Hole on the Flute examines the life, work and traveling of Romany musicians in the far south of the Balkans.
Strangely in an age that prizes gender-neutral language, that is the literal plural of "Rom", a Romany word meaning man or husband.
He pointed out that a Romany musician was also a regular guest on the show.
But not all Gypsies are dark-skinned and only about 4m, at most, speak some kind of Romany.
